records.ety: [Prob. fr. hang; cf. D. hunkeren, hengelen.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hankered (-krd); p. pr. & vb. n. Hankering.]

defs: To long (for) with a keen appetite and uneasiness; to have a vehement desire; -- usually with for or after; as, to hanker after fruit; to hanker after the diversions of the town.
quotes: He was hankering to join his friend.

defs: To linger in expectation or with desire.
